target.name: Hailo
target.founder: Orr Danon, Hadar Zeitlin, Avi Baum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $336M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Hailo is a Series D semiconductor company that has raised $336M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors, including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, and Cerebras at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy base, applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $504M. The peer-median method anchors to 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $2.7B (range: $529M to $5.0B), reflecting wide dispersion in the frontier-chip sector. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales cannot be applied; target revenue is not disclosed. 

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $428M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around Hailo's revenue generation, competitive positioning within a fragmented AI-chip market, and the outsized valuations commanded by leading peers like SambaNova. Stage-progression suggests more conservative positioning; peer medians anchor higher. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, time-to-scale, and capital efficiency relative to the $2.7B–$5.0B peer cohort.
